tier_0.title: Minor style: single-letter variable name
tier_0.summary: Variable 'x' used in loop body — acceptable in this context
tier_0.rationale: Advisory only — human explicitly accepted this style in codebase
tier_0.suggested_fix: Rename x to item if desired, but not required

tier_1.title: Dead import in cli.py
tier_1.summary: os.path imported but never used in cli.py
tier_1.rationale: Targeted dead-code removal with localized user-visible impact — audit follow-up scope
tier_1.severity_reason: Clutters namespace; ruff would flag this
tier_1.evidence: cli.py:3 — import os.path (no usages found by grep)

tier_1.title: Missing error path test for validate command
tier_1.summary: validate command has no test for the case where epic JSON is malformed
tier_1.rationale: Targeted test addition with concrete user impact (unhandled exception) — audit follow-up scope
tier_1.severity_reason: Malformed epic JSON would produce an unhandled exception in production
tier_1.evidence: Test Gaps section of auditor report — malformed epic scenario untested
tier_1.suggested_fix: Add test_validate_malformed_epic that writes a truncated JSON and asserts exit code 1

tier_2.title: normalize_epic missing schema version field
tier_2.summary: Epic JSON has no version field making future migrations harder
tier_2.rationale: Architectural addition — needs planning and migration script, beyond worker scope
tier_2.severity_reason: Future schema migrations will require scanning all epic files without a version sentinel
tier_2.evidence: models.py:28-53 — normalize_epic has no version field in defaults
tier_2.suggested_fix: Add version: 1 to normalize_epic defaults and document the field in a schema changelog
